text: The approach is via a left thoracotomy.
The ligamentum arteriosum is ligated and divided, which effectively divides the vascular ring.
The distal aortic arch and diverticulum are dissected out.
The left carotid artery is then dissected out and exposed for later use.
It is then encircled with a vessel loop.
After being dissected out, a partial occlusion clamp is applied to the distal aortic arch to include the diverticulum.
The diverticulum is cut off the aorta along with the left subclavian artery which is controlled with a vascular bulldog clamp.
The aorta is proximally over sown in two layers.
Note the thin wall of the diverticulum.
The clamp is then removed from the aorta and there is some trivial bleeding from the suture line which is easily controlled.
Here, the diverticulum is removed from the left subclavian artery.
This includes the stump of the ligamentum arteriosum.
The exposed left carotid artery is now controlled with a vascular clamp.
A longitudinal arteriotomy is performed and the left subclavian artery is anastomosed or attached to the left carotid with fine polypropylene suture.
The clamp has now been removed and the anastomosis is seen here, complete.
The pleural is closed once hemostasis is obtained and the incision is closed in a routine manner.
